title: Integrated Intermediate Modeling Capstone · Decision Notebook: Model Governance
summary: Build a decision model from messy requirements, data, constraints, and alternatives. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math int model governance increment in a stakeholder-ready computational modeling portfolio. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Document data provenance, revisions, approvals, and conditions requiring model retirement.
Explain methods and limitations with equations, code, visuals, and plain-language implications.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Calculate Model Governance evidence
steps.instruction: A measured quantity starts at 22 units, changes by −9 units, then by +8 units. Calculate the final quantity. Use the result to decide Model Governance for a stakeholder-ready computational modeling portfolio.
steps.why: The Model Governance result for a stakeholder-ready computational modeling portfolio is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Preserve the signs and compute 22 − 9 + 8; compare with the starting scale. Reconcile the result with this competency evidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Document data provenance, revisions, approvals, and conditions requiring model retirement.
